Best Fit: Lowest-change rehost and legacy runtime parity
Identity And Secrets: Managed identity plus Key Vault possible
Operational Trade-Off: Most control but highest patching and operations burden

Operational Model: PaaS web app hosting for one or more containers
Best Fit: Simple web apps that need platform-managed TLS and deployment slots
Scaling: Scale out by instance count or autoscale rules
Identity And Secrets: Managed identity and app settings/Key Vault references
Operational Trade-Off: Easy operations but less Kubernetes-like portability

Operational Model: Serverless managed containers on Kubernetes foundations
Best Fit: Containerized monoliths and microservices needing ingress revisions and scale-to-zero style operations
column9: Good balance of managed operations and container-native features; less cluster control than AKS

Operational Model: Managed Kubernetes control plane with user-managed cluster operations
Best Fit: Complex platforms needing Kubernetes APIs and ecosystem
Scaling: Horizontal pod autoscaler and cluster autoscaler
Networking: Deep network policy and CNI choices
Identity And Secrets: Workload identity and external secrets patterns
Operational Trade-Off: Most flexible but highest platform engineering responsibility
